entry.title: ISYS2421 Final Exam

entry.title: ISYS2421 Sample Exam
entry.content:
130分， 14道题， 3章， 3小时


A: Theory [40 分总]

(1 x 30 MCQ + 2 x 3 Short Answer)

A1: General

(2 + 2 = 4 Marks)

1.1 What is the difference between data and information?
s20a:改为MCQ

Data: stored representations of objects and events that have meaning and importance in the user’s environment, however without proper processing its merely raw or plain characters which still meaningless.

information: is meaningful and is data that have been processed in such a way as to increase the knowledge of the person who uses the data.

Metadata: are data that describe the properties or characteristics of end-user data and the context of that data.


1.2 Why are databases important?
s20a:改为MCQ

Database is integral component of all information systems and most websites, and almost all software development involves working with a database. And most of the databases can be managed by similar Structured Query Language


A2: Terms

(1 + 1 + 1 + 1 = 4 Marks)
What do the following acronyms stand for?

2.1.1 SQL
s20a:原题
Structured Query Language

2.1.2 OLAP
s20a:原题
On-Line Analytical Processing, the use of a set of graphical tools that provides users with multidimensional views of their data and allows them to analyze the data using simple windowing techniques. OLAP is also part of descriptive analytics; it allows users to get a multidimensional view of data and drill down deeper to the details when appropriate and useful.


2.2 What is a Primary Key?

s20a:原题
The Primary key is the unique identifier of the field, and can be 1 or more fields.
The Primary key is an attribute or a combination of attributes that uniquely identifies each row in a relation.


2.3 What is a Foreign Key?

s20a:原题
A Foreign Key is an attribute in one entity that is a primary key in another entity, and its establish the relationship between two entities


2.4 What is a DBA?

DBA: Database Administrators are responsible for physical database design and for managing technical issues in the database environment.
A database administrator: a person who is responsible for physical database design and for dealing with the technical issues—such as security enforcement, database performance, and backup and recovery—associated with managing a database
DBA: Database administration, A technical function that is responsible for physical database design and for dealing with technical issues, such as security enforcement, database performance, and backup and recovery.
DA: Data administrators are persons who are responsible for the overall management of data resources in an organization.
DA: Data administration, A high-level function that is responsible for the overall management of data resources in an organization, including maintaining corporate-wide definitions and standards.
A data administrator: a person who takes overall responsibility for data, metadata, and policies about data use


A3: Modelling

(2 + 2 = 4 Marks)

3.1 Describe the various cardinalities of relationships allowed in an Entity Relationship Diagram.

s20a:分解为3道MCQ

Cardinality: A rule that specifies the number of instances of one entity that can (or must) be associated with each instance of another entity.


3.1.1 What is One-to-One s20a:MCQ

One instance from one entity that can be associated with only one instance from another entity. For example, one student can have only one student card.


3.1.2 What is One-to-Many s20a:MCQ

One instance from one entity that can be associated with many instance from another entity. For example, one customer can place many order.


3.1.3 What is Many-to-Many s20a:MCQ

Multiple instance from one entity that can be associated with multiple instance from another entity. For example, many order can have many products and many products can be mentioned in many order.


3.2 Often, an entity can become a relationship and a relationship (in Many to Many Relationship) can be re-drawn as an entity. Explain what this means and give an example to illustrate your explanation.

When there is a binary many to many relationships between two entity, a separate relation need to be created, and the primary key of this separate relation is a composite key, consisting primary key of each of the entities that participate in the relationship. And if such relationships are not essential for the business purpose, this separate entity can be simplified as a linking relationship between two entities.


A4: Anomalies & Normalisation

(2 + 2 + 2 = 6 Marks)

4.1.1 What is Anomalies [异常]

An inconsistency or error that can result when attempts to update a database table that contains redundant data.


4.1.2 Describe a deletion anomaly [删除异常] s20a and give an example. (2分).

The deletion anomaly occurs when deleting one record leads to accidental deletion of other unrelated records or needs to delete multiple duplicated records in order to complete a single record deletion task.

For example, deletion of one customer leads to lost of all its historical records of transaction.


4.1.3 What is Insert Anomaly? [插入异常]s20a

The insert anomaly happens when inserting data from one entity, require insert data from another entity, and need to insert new value more than once.

For example, the university requires adding a new curriculum, but before inserting any student records, it can not add a record for that new curriculum and its coordinator.


4.1.4 What is Update Anomaly? [更新异常] s20a

The update anomaly happens when modifying or changing one recorded value requires to repeat a similar operation multiple times on modifying other duplicated copies, or one change leads to many inconsistencies in data records and damaging data integrity.

For example, the company requires to modify the price of a single product, that requires to change all the transaction that related to this product.


4.2.0 Functional Dependency [函数依赖]

Functional dependency A constraint between two attributes in which the value of one attribute is determined by the value of another attribute.
A relationship between two attributes, one field is dependant on another, and one can not exists without another.

（学生ID，所修课程ID）→ 成绩, 成绩既不能单独依赖于学生ID，也不能单独依赖于所修课程ID，因此成绩完全函数依赖于关键字


4.2.1 Describe a Transitive Dependency [传递函数依赖], give an example. (2分)

Transitive Dependency can create unnecessary redundancy, and is a functional dependency between the primary key and one or more non-key attributes that are dependent on the primary key via another non-key attribute.


4.2.2 Describe a Partial Dependency [部分函数依赖] s20a

a field or attribute that on part of the primary key or candidate key.

or, A functional dependency in which one or more non-key attributes are functionally dependent on part, but not all of the primary key.


4.3 Describe what CASCADE DELETE means and when it should be used. (2分)

Cascade delete, remove entire record and any related foreign keys.


4.4 referential constraint 引用约束 s20a

A referential integrity constraint is a rule that maintains consistency among the rows of two relations. The rule states that if there is a foreign key in one relation, either each foreign key value must match a primary key value in another relation or the foreign key value must be null.

when updating a primary key value, all related foreign key needs to be when updating a primary key value, all related foreign key need to be cascaded update, or we can simply disallow updates of primary key.

when deleting a foreign key, the primary key must not be affected by that, which means disallow deletion of the primary key, and after the deletion of the foreign key record, the null value must be assigned to substitute those record.


A5: Business Rules

5.1 Describe the different ways business rules can be implemented.

Business Rule can be shown as:
1. show in Data Model, ERD & RM.
2. SQL Constraint or Referential Constraints, such as set up the primary Key and foreign Key constraint to constrain the relationship and the data flow. and also, auto incremental, cascade delete, etc., As well as through the data type, variable length.
3. Trigger, trigger what happen when the IUD of the data not meet the criteria
4. English, okay all enough but still the business rule not show, write a plint language or a code to explain.


5.2 Discuss how a business rule that is likely to change in the future should be implemented.

do not enforce business rule at the database structure design level
business rule can be implemented more easier at program level by implement it as a library function. By doing so only one function need to be modified if there is a change in business rule. and its can be a cost saving way.


A6: SQL

(2 + 2 + 2 = 6 Marks)

6.1.1 What is an INDEX ?

Index: is a table or other data structure used to determine in a file the location of records that satisfy some condition.

6.1.2 Index can slow down: Updates.
6.1.3 Index can speed up: Search and Joining operations.


b) Describe what a “trigger” object is within a database system, give an example of a trigger and how it can be used (code not necessary).

A named set of SQL statements that are considered (triggered) when a data modification (i.e., INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) occurs or if certain data definitions are encountered. If a condition stated within a trigger is met, then a prescribed action is taken.


c) What is the difference between a stored procedure and a function ?

Function: A stored subroutine that returns one value and has only input parameters.
Procedure: A collection of procedural and SQL statements that are assigned a unique name within the schema and stored in the database.
Stored Procedures: are modules of code that implement application logic and are included on the database server.


A7: Database Issues

(2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 = 12 Marks)

7.1 Why is the DATA in a database the most valuable thing on a computer server ?

Hardware can be renewed, data lost is a not reversible disaster.


7.2 What are some critical issues to consider in a backup plan ?

Data is the most valuable things that needs to be backed-up, a full backup or partial backup of differential and incremental only? frequency and period of the backup.


7.3 One of the primary ways of distributing data is replication, describe what it is.
Replication [复制]: is a Distribution Mechanisms that Keep multiple copies of the same data on each physical server and update at regular intervals, and Must accept that not all data will always be exactly up-to-date


7.4 Replication Types

Snapshot: take periodic copies of a table or parts
Real Time Updates, as each transaction is made, send transaction details to other sites to update as well. Frequency Would depend on needs and comms speed.
Push or Pull, Can be controlled by the originating server (Push) or Receiving server (Pull)


7.5 five advantages to data replication:

7.5.1 Reliability If one of the sites containing the relation (or database) fails, a copy can always be found at another site without network traffic delays. Also, available copies can all be updated as soon as transactions occur, and unavailable nodes will be updated once they return to service.

7.5.2 Fast response Each site that has a full copy can process queries locally, so queries can be processed rapidly.

7.5.3 Possible avoidance of complicated distributed transaction integrity routines Replicated databases are usually refreshed at scheduled intervals, so most forms of replication are used when some relaxing of synchronization across database copies is acceptable.

7.5.4 Node decoupling Each transaction may proceed without coordination across the network. Thus, if nodes are down, busy, or disconnected (e.g., in the case of mobile personal computers), a transaction is handled when the user desires. In the place of real-time synchronization of updates, a behind-the-scenes process coordinates all data copies.

7.5.5 Reduced network traffic at prime time Often updating data happens during prime business hours, when network traffic is highest and the demands for rapid response greatest. Replication, with delayed updating of copies of data, mov


7.6 three disadvantages: to data replication:

7.6.1 Storage requirements Each site that has a full copy must have the same storage capacity that would be required if the data were stored centrally. Each copy requires storage space (the cost for which is constantly decreasing), and processing time is required to update each copy on each node.

7.6.2 Complexity related to maintaining database integrity Unless very costly mechanisms for maintaining identical copies of the database in real time are used, it is essential to ensure that potential discrepancies between the copies do not lead to business problems caused by inconsistent data. This requires potentially complex coordination requirements at the application level. This may cause undesirable coupling between the database and applications.

7.6.3 Complexity and cost of updating Whenever a relation is updated, it must (eventually) be updated at each site that holds a copy. Synchronizing updating in nearreal-time can require careful coordination, as will be clear later under the topic of commit protocol.


7.7 In relation to Client Server architectures, what are the advantages and disadvantages between using a shared MS-Access MDB file on a file server and using a full DBMS running on a server ?

The MS-Access MDB is the file of database that transferred between the authorized user, which means ever time the entire file will been transferred forth and back, this can be time consuming if the data grow larger. But normally once download of the file the query data management and analysis task can be done locally. Besides the Microsoft Access is the database file, which will transfer through the Server Message Block [伺服器訊息區塊] (SMB), to share the file, which produced by the Microsoft, or this may require the protocol such as the File Transfer Protocol [文件传输协议] (FTP) and via the server will become a File Server [文件服务器] (FS), and the critical issue to consider when use the file server is the storage method, the storage capacity, the security and authority control, efficiency of the data storing. This point to the short come of using the file based database transfer, is the slow due to the file size and the data integrity problem which can probably causes by the non-sync CURD of the data.

The full DBMS running on a server, on the other hand the client do not need to download entire database, the client only sent the query to get the records that they need, which the result transfer is done more quicker than the then entire file downloading. Yet, as the query frequency increased (e.g. a BANK may have many transactions occur during a business hour every time to do that CRUD with the database need to connect with the server, which the efficiency can be decreased)


What are Text Databases s20a:改为 MongoDB used for ?

The clear leader in the document store category (and also the most popular NoSQL database management system in general) is MongoDB, also an open source product. MongoDB offers a broader range of capabilities than Redis and is not as strongly focused solely on performance. MongoDB offers versatile indexing, high availability through automated replication, a query mechanism, its own file system for storing large objects, and automatic sharding for distributing the processing load between multiple servers. It does not, however, support joins or transactions. Instead of JSON, MongoDB uses BSON as its storage format. BSON is a binary JSON-like structure that is designed to be easy and quick to traverse and fast to encode and decode. In practice, this means that it is easier and faster to find things within a BSON structure than within a JSON structure.


7.8 Where does the data stored in a DataWarehouse come from and how might it be modified before being stored into the DataWarehouse ?

Data warehouse: A subject-oriented, integrated, time-variant, nonupdateable collection of data used in support of management decision-making processes.

Data are extracted from the various internal and external source system files and databases.

The data from the various source systems are transformed and integrated before being loaded into the data marts. Transactions may be sent to the source systems to correct errors discovered in data staging. The data warehouse is considered to be the collection of data marts.


8.extra

8.1 Unary relationship: is a relationship between the instances of a single entity type. (Unary relationships are also called recursive relationships.)
8.2 Binary relationship: A relationship between the instances of two entity types.
8.3 Ternary relationship: A simultaneous relationship among the instances of three entity types.
8.4 NoSQL: A category of recently introduced data storage and retrieval technologies that are not based on the relational model.
8.5 Descriptive analytics: Describes the past status of the domain of interest using a variety of tools through techniques such as reporting, data visualization, dashboards, and scorecards.
8.6 Predictive analytics: Applies statistical and computational methods and models to data regarding past and current events to predict what might happen in the future.
8.7 Prescriptive analytics: Uses results of predictive analytics together with optimization and simulation tools to recommend actions that will lead to a desired outcome.
8.8 Normalization: The process of decomposing relations with anomalies to produce smaller, well-structured relations.
8.9 First normal form: Any multivalued attributes (also called repeating groups) have been removed, so there is a single value (possibly null) at the intersection of each row and column of the table (as in Figure 4-2b).
8.10 Second normal form: Any partial functional dependencies have been removed (i.e., nonkey attributes are identified by the whole primary key).
8.11 Third normal form: Any transitive dependencies have been removed (i.e., nonkey attributes are identified by only the primary key).
8.12 Boyce-Codd normal: form Any remaining anomalies that result from functional dependencies have been removed (because there was more than one possible primary key for the same nonkeys).
8.13 Fourth normal form: Any multivalued dependencies have been removed.
8.14 Fifth normal form: Any remaining anomalies have been removed.



B: SQL [50 分总]

s20a:[40 分总]

CD Express (a CD mail order company) has implemented the following tables:

  1. Entity Relationship Diagram:

  1. Relational Model:
CUSTOMERCUSTORDERORDER_ITEMITEMCOURIERSTAFF
Customer_NoOrder_NoOrder_No+Item_NoCourier_NoStaff_No
NameOrderDateItem_NoNameNameName
AddressOrderTimeQuantityPriceChargeJoined
SuburbDeliveryDateSizeResigned
PostcodeDeliveryCompleted
Date_of_BirthCustomer_No
PackedBy_StaffNo
CheckBy_StaffNo
Courier_No
  1. Assumptions:
  • Size is in Kilograms (Kg),
  • DeliveryCompleted is specified as Y-yes and N-no,
  • Price is in Australian dollars,
  • Delivery address is assumed to be the customer address details,
  • Charge by courier is per item delivered (not by weight – courier assumes all packages small because their only CDs).

Answer the following questions in relation to the previous described data model.

B1: View

(9 Marks)

A query to create a view that includes all orders to be delivered today. The view must contain the following fields: customer name, name of courier, courier charge, name of staff who checked the order and the order date.


B2: Group By

(9 Marks)

For each order, show how many items the order contains (using the Qty field). For extra marks, calculate the total cost of the order as well.


B3: Nested

(7 Marks)

Provide a list of Items that have NEVER BEEN SOLD.

OR


B4: Data Definition

(3+2+1+1=7 Marks)

a) Specify the query to CREATE the COURIER table (making appropriate assumptions about the field types). Implement the primary key constraint.

b) Specify a query to update the ITEM record, of item_no 50004, to a price of 11.50.

c) Specify a query to insert the following cage into the CUSTOMER table:

Customer_NoNameAddressSuburbPostcodeDate_of_birth
6Eric Vike32 Rugged RoadLambohov583331974-Apr-28

d) Specify a query to delete item number 50010 from the ITEM table:


B5: Query Output

(2+2+2+3+3+3+3=18 Marks)

Show output of the following queries, include the column heading, based on the data shown later.

5.1

NameSuburb
Hendry DJolimont
Martin AGeelong
Vince FGeelong

5.2

namename
KangarooMarsupial
ParrotBird

5.3

namename
DragonFlyHendry D
MosquitoHendry D

5.4

namecount(*)
Martin A2
Arthur B3
Hendry D2

5.5

name
Ross E

5.6

KeeperNoName
K5Ross E
K6Vince F

5.7 s20a:SELECT from 4 Options

Keeper_nameSupervisor_name
Arthur BMartin A
David CArthur B
Hendry DMartin A
Ross EDavid C
Vince FDavid C

SAMPLE DATA FOR SOME OF THE TABLES IN THE SECTION B DATA MODEL


SPECIES

SpeciesNoNameEndangered
S1CanineN
S2BirdY
S3MarsupialN

S5InsectY

KEEPER

KeeperNoNameAddressSuburbPostcodeSupervisor No
K1Martin A64 Smith StreetGeelong3000NULL
K2Arthur B16 Smith RoadSt. Kilda3012K1

ANIMAL

AnimalNoNameDateOfBirthPlaceOfBirthCageNoSpeciesNoVet_KeeperNo
32100Collie9-feb-2003GeelongC110S1K1
32101BullTerri9-feb-2003EppingC106S1K2
32102Kangaroo10-feb-2003SheppartonC100S3K3
32103DragonFly11-feb-2004GeelongC105S5K4

C1001 Scary Way10x3x2016:00K5

C1052 Wasp Street80x10x10009:30K5

C: Data Modelling [40 分总]

C1:

(5 + 5 = 10 Marks)

1.1 What is wrong with the following set of tables, designed for a small boat hire company ?

BOATCUSTOMER
Boat_NoCustomer_Name
Boat_NameAddress
Hire_DatePostcode
Hire_TimePhone
Customer_NameState
Passenger_CapacityAge
Number_Of_Passengers

1.2 How would you redesign them (show revised ERD and Relational Model of travel agent tables) ?

Note: you may answer both of these parts, to question C1, (1.1 & 1.2) together. Feel free to use the space below or on the next page.

  1. Entity Relationship Diagram (Note: Attributes do NOT need to be shown):
  1. Relational Model

C2:

Some of your friends want to set up a new website to sell products for children over the internet called KidsWorld. They have asked you to design a database to be used by the website to keep details of the products and also to record orders that are received from customers via the web site.

They are planning to sell two major types of products for kids: toys and DVDs. For the toys, they will just need to store a description of the toy as well as the retail price. For DVDs they will need a description and price, but will also need a rating (eg G, PG etc) and a running time, so these can be displayed on the website. For each product (both toys and DVDs), a count will be needed to keep track of how many copies of the product are currently in stock.

Some of the toys and DVDs are individual, but many are part of a larger theme. For example, with Toy Story 3 about to be released, they are planning to have a large stock of Toy Story toys and DVDs. They want the website users to be able to search for products based on the theme they are part of (eg. List all toys and DVDs by the Wiggles).

When customers choose to purchase toys/DVDs, the order will need to be stored into the database, as well as details of the customer. Naturally this will include details such as name, address and phone number. The customer will also specify the delivery address for the order, as this may be different to their billing address.

Customers will be required to pay before the products are sent so details will need to be kept of the payment. Your friends are planning to accept payment by credit card, direct bank deposit and paypal. For credit cards they need to store the credit card number and expiry date, for direct deposit they need a field to tick off that the payment has appeared in their bank account and for paypal they again need a field to tick off plus the paypal user id of the payer.

Each order placed by a customer can be for multiple products so you will need to account for this.

Provide the following for the new database:

  • Entity Relationship Diagram.
  • Third Normal Form Relational Model (showing fields, primary keys, foreign keys and concatenated keys).
  • Justify the choices you have made. Are the entities and relationships you have

chosen free of problems ? What assumptions have you made ?

  1. Entity Relationship Diagram (Note: Attributes do NOT need to be shown)

  2. Relational Model

  1. Business Rules/Assumptions

entry.title: INTE2047 Final Exam
entry.summary: Here's something encrypted, password is required to continue reading.

entry.title: RO 段落排序
entry.content:
001Indian IT 真题
1Innovation in India is as much due to entrepreneurialism as it is to IT skills, says Arun Maria, chairman of Boston Consulting Group in India.
2Indian businessmen have used IT to create new business models that enable them to provide services in a more cost-effective way.
3This is not something that necessarily requires expensive technical research.
4He suggests the country’s computer services industry can simply outsource research to foreign universities if the capability is not available locally.
5“This way, I will have access to the best scientists in the world without having to produce them myself” says Mr. Maria.

002Foreign aid 真题
1But beginning in the 1990s, foreign aid had begun to slowly improve.
2Scrutiny by the news media shamed many developed countries into curbing their bad practices.
3Today, the projects of organizations like the World Bank are meticulously inspected by watchdog groups.
4Although the system is far from perfect, it is certainly more transparent than it was when foreign aid routinely helped ruthless dictators stay in power.

003Jet Stream 真题
1Jet stream, narrow, swift currents or tubes of air found at heights ranging from 7 to 8 mi (11.3–12.9 km) above the surface of the earth.
2They are caused by great temperature differences between adjacent air masses. There are four major jet streams.
3Instead of moving along a straight line, the jet stream flows in a wavelike fashion; the waves propagate eastward (in the Northern Hemisphere) at speeds considerably slower than the wind speed itself.
4Since the progress of an airplane is aided or impeded depending on whether tail winds or head winds are encountered, in the Northern Hemisphere the jet stream is sought by eastbound aircraft, in order to

004Map 真题
1For as long as I can remember, there has been a map in the ticket hall of Piccadilly Circus tube station supposedly showing night and day across the time zones of the world.
2This is somewhat surprising given the London Underground’s historic difficultly in grasping the concept of punctuality.
3But this map has always fascinated me, and still does, even though it now seems very primitive.
4This is because it chops the world up equally by longitude, without regard the reality of either political divisions or the changing seasons.

005Chimpanzees 真题
1A simple way to disprove this hypothesis (the Innateness Hypothesis) is to demonstrate that other species have the capacity to speak but for some reason simply have not developed speech.
2A logical candidate for such a species is the chimpanzee, which shares 98.4% of the human genetic code.
3Chimpanzees cannot speak because, unlike homo sapiens, their vocal cords are located higher in their throats and cannot be controlled as delicately as human vocal cords.
4It does not follow from their lack of speech, however, that chimpanzees are incapable of language.
5Perhaps they can acquire grammar like humans if they could only express it some other way.

006Immigration Effect 真题
1In the early years of the twenty-first century the impact of immigrants on the welfare state and, specifically, the capacity of the welfare state to absorb large numbers of immigrants has become a staple of discussion among policy makers and politicians.
2It is also a recurrent theme in the press, from the highbrow pages of Prospect to the populism of the Daily Mail.
3Inevitably, these discussions focus on present-day dilemmas.
4But the issues themselves are not new and have historical roots that go much deeper than have been acknowledged

007Mother of Storm 真题
1Unlike Barnes’ previous books, Mother of Storms has a fairly large cast of viewpoint characters.
2This usually irritates me, but I didn’t mind it here, and their interactions are well-handled and informative, although occasionally in moving them about the author’s manipulations are a bit blatant.
3They’re not all necessarily good guys, either, although with the hurricanes wreaking wholesale destruction upon the world’s coastal areas, ethical categories tend to become irrelevant.
4But even the Evil American Corporate Magnate is a pretty likable guy.

008Charles Lindbergh 真题
1After finishing first in his pilot training class, Lindbergh took his first job as the chief pilot of an airmail route operated by Robertson Aircraft Co. of Lambert Field in St. Louis, Missouri.
2He flew the mail in a de Havilland DH-4 biplane to Springfield, Illinois, Peoria and Chicago.
3During his tenure on the mail route, he was renowned for delivering the mail under any circumstances.
4After a crash, he even salvaged bags of mail from his burning aircraft and immediately phoned Alexander Varney, Peoria’s airport manager, to advise him to send a truck.

009SEPAHUA 真题
1SEPAHUA, a ramshackle town on the edge of Peru’s Amazon jungle, nestles in a pocket on the map where a river of the same name flows into the Urubamba.
2That pocket denotes a tiny patch of legally loggable land sandwiched between four natural reserves, all rich in mahogany and accessible from the town. “Boundaries are on maps,” says a local logger, “maps
3In 2001 the government, egged on by WWF, a green group, tried to regulate logging in the relatively small part of the Peruvian Amazon where this is allowed.
4It abolished the previous system of annual contracts.
5Instead, it auctioned 40-year concessions to areas ruled off on a map, with the right to log 5% of the area each year. The aim was to encourage strict management plans and sustainable extraction.

011International Date Line 真题
1International Date Line, imaginary line on the earth’s surface, generally following the 180° meridian of longitude, where, by international agreement, travelers change dates.
2The date line is necessary to avoid a confusion that would otherwise result.
3For example, if an airplane were to travel westward with the sun, 24 hr would elapse as it circled the globe, but it would still be the same day for those in the airplane while it would be one day latefor
4The same problem would arise if two travelers journeyed in opposite directions to a point on the opposite side of the earth, 180° of longitude distant.
5The apparent paradox is resolved by requiring that the traveler crossing the date line change his date, thus bringing the travelers into agreement when they meet.

012Aviation 真题
1During the 1920s and 1930s great progress was made in the field of aviation, including the first transatlantic flight of Alcock and Brown in 1919, Charles Lindbergh’s solo transatlantic flight in 1927, and Charles Kingsford Smith’s transpacific flight the following year.
2One of the most successful designs of this period was the Douglas DC-3, which became the first airliner to be profitable carrying passengers
3By the beginning of World War II, many towns and cities had built airports, and there were numerous qualified pilots available.
4The war brought many innovations to aviation, including the first jet aircraft and the first liquid-fueled rockets.

013Aviation after World War II 真题

3The researchers claim to have collected information over a period of two years from sources including over 500 published contents and 80 experts in the BCGI’s network.
4The data will be kept updated with the discovery of new species or the extinction of some, said the researchers. All the data required for the world’s trees is now available in one database, thanks to
5The results of the research were published in the Journal of Sustainable Forestry.
6Actually, 60,050 species ……

081Monash Student Ne Tan 真题
1Mechanical engineering student Ne Tan is spending the first semester of this year studying at the University of California, Berkeley as part of the Monash Abroad program.
2Ne, an international student from Shanghai, China, began her Monash journey at Monash College in October 2006.
3There she completed a diploma that enabled her to enter Monash University as a secondyear student.
4Now in her third year of study, the Monash Abroad program will see her complete four units of study in the US before returning to Australia in May 2009.

082Voice higher than 5mhz 非原文
1A study showed man can not hear voice higher than 5 hertz …
2To test this theory, xxx from xxx university gathered 6 students …
3As in the previous study, the volunteers cannot hear any sound higher than 5 hertz
4In thought of … as this frequency is too high that …

083Sojourner 真题
1More recent missions to Mars include the hugely successful Mars Pathfinder, which landed a small `rover’ called Sojourner on the surface to explore a region where there may once have been life.
2Sojourner has now been effectively switched off, but lasted almost twelve times its expected lifetime.
3Similarly, the lander, which imaged several areas around the landing site (dubbed the Carl Sagan Memorial site) and took atmospheric measurements, lasted a good deal longer than expected.
4The only unfortunate thing to have arisen from the mission is the naming of the rocks at the landing site (including everything from Scooby Doo to Darth Vader)

084Sustainable Development 真题
1Whatever happened to the idea of progress and a better future? I still believe both
2The Brundtland Report, our Common Future (1987) defines sustainable development as” development which meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own
3Implicit in this definition is the idea that the old pattern of development could not be sustained. Is this true?
4Development in the past was driven by growth and innovation. It led to new technologies and huge improvements in living standards.
5To assume that we know what the circumstances or needs of future generations will be is mistaken and inevitably leads to the debilitating sense that we are living on borrowed time.

086Bankruptcy 真题
1In Montana as elsewhere, companies that have acquired older mines respond to demands to pay for cleanup in either of two ways.
2Especially if the company is small, its owners may declare the company bankrupt, in some cases conceal its assets, and transfer their business efforts to other companies or to new companies that do nobear
3If the company is so large that it cannot claim that it would be bankrupted by cleanup costs, the company instead denies its responsibility or else seeks to minimize the costs.
4In either case, either the mine site and areas downstream of it remain toxic, thereby endangering people, or else the U.S. federal government and the Montana state government pay for the cleanup through the federal Superfund and a corresponding Montana state fund.

087Historical Records 真题
1Historical records, coins, and other date-bearing objects can help – if they exist. But even prehistoric sites contain records – written in nature’s hand.
2The series of strata in an archaeological dig enables an excavator to date recovered objects relatively, if not absolutely.
3However, when archaeologists want know the absolute date of a site, they can often go beyond simple stratigraphy.
4For example, tree rings, Dendrochronology (literally, te of a site, they cooden artefacts by matching their ring patterns to known records, which, in some areas of the world, span several thousand years.

088Music record in Brazil 真题
1Early in 1938, one Folklore Research Mission dispatched to the north-eastern hinterlands of Brazil on a similar mission.
2His intention was to record as much music as possible as quickly as possible, before encroaching influences like radio and cinema began transforming the region’s distinctive culture.
3They recorded whoever and whatever seemed to be interesting: piano carriers, cowboys, beggars, voodoo priests, quarry workers, fishermen, dance troupes and even children at play.
4Regrettably, the Brazilian mission’s collection ended up languishing in vaults here.

089Easier said than Done 真题
1In ‘Easier Said than Done’, we set out some of the reasons why we might find it hard to live in a healthy way, exercising, eating well, getting adequate sleep, and checking for early warning symptoms.
2Perhaps most importantly, we look to the field of behavioural science for strategies that people can use to overcome those hurdles and to initiate lifestyle changes.
3These include Commitment devices, where we make it very unattractive to not follow through on an intention.
4Changing existing behaviour can be a difficult task, but with the help of these strategies new behaviours can become habitual, facilitating a long-term sustained healthy lifestyle.

092Science and technology 真题
1It is a truism to say that in 21st century society science and technology are important.
2Human existence in the developed world is entirely dependent on some fairly recent developments in science and technology.
3Whether this is good or bad is, of course, up for argument
4But the fact that science underlies our lives, our health, our work, our communications, our entertainment and our transport is undeniable.

093Accounting System 真题
1Are there any systems that can measure the Accounting system?
2Well, there is accounting software describes a type of application software that records and processes accounting transactions within functional modules such as accounts payable, accounts receivable,
3This enables the access anywhere at any time with any device which is Internet enabled, or may be desktop based. It varies greatly in its complexity and cost.
4These tools combine together to provide quality customer service and create a climate of confidence, a customer service strategy that helps meet the specific needs.

094Earthquake in San Francisco 真题
1At 5:12 a.m. on April 18, 1906, the people of San Francisco were awakened by an earthquake that would devastate the city.
2The main temblor, having a 7.7–7.9 magnitude, lasted about one minute and was the result of the rupturing of the northernmost 296 miles of the 800-mile San Andreas fault.
3But when calculating destruction, the earthquake took second place to the great fire that followed.
4The fire, lasting four days, most likely started with broken gas lines (and, in some cases, was helped along by people hoping to collect insurance for their property—they were covered for fire, but not

095Games affect brains 真题
1Palaus and his colleagues wanted to see if any trends had emerged from the research to date concerning how video games affect the structure and activity of our brains.
2They collected the results from 116 scientific studies, 22 of which looked at structural changes in the brain and 100 of which looked at changes in brain functionality and/or behavior.
3The studies show that playing video games can change how our brains perform, and even their structure.
4For example, playing video games affects our attention, and some studies found that gamers show improvements in several types of attention, such as sustained attention or selective attention.

096Copernicus’s Heliocentric Theory 真题
1Copernicus probably hit upon his main idea sometime between 1508 and 1514.
2For years, however, he delayed publication of his controversial work, which contradicted all the authorities of the time.
3The historic book that contains the final version of his theory, De Revolutionibus Orbium Coelestium Libri VI (“Six Books Concerning the Revolutions of the Heavenly Orbs”), did not appear in print until
4According to legend, Copernicus received a copy as he was dying, on May 24, 1543.
5The book opened the way to a truly scientific approach to astronomy. It had a profound influence on later thinkers of the scientific revolution, including such major figures as Galileo, Johannes Keplerand

036Ancestors lit fire 非原文
1Many years ago, humans had been eating raw meat only.
2(to be confirmed)
3Then they learned how to light a fire.
4Since then humans started eating cooked meat.
5And they also learned to cook many other food.

068Martin Luther King 非原文
1Rose Parks has a great impact on the civil rights movements.
2She refused to give her bus seat to a white man.
3The bus driver arrested her.
4Her arrested was … by Martin Luther King.
5King then … a boycott on the bus system.

097Music and Language 非原文
1Language can convey message
2Especially written language
3Music was conveyed orally only, until the 11th century when physical instruments were invented to perform music.
4It was hard to teach music.
5But now it’s easy.

098Fibers for clothing 非原文
1Fibers suitable for clothing have been made for the first time from the wheat protein gluten.
2The fibers are as strong and soft as wool and silk.
3However, up to 30 times cheaper.
4Narendra Reddy and Yiqi Yang, who produced the fibers at the University of Nebraska in Lincoln. He says that because they are biodegradable, they might be used in biomedical applications such as surgical

099Native English Speaker 非原文
1Anyone wanting to get to the top of international business, medicine or academia (but possibly not sport) needs to be able to speak English to a pretty high level.
2Equally, any native English speaker wanting to deal with these new high achievers needs to know how to talk without baffling them.
3Because so many English-speakers today are monoglots, they have little idea how difficult it is to master another language.
4Many think the best way to make foreigners understand is to be chatty and informal.
5This may seem friendly but, as it probably involves using colloquial expressions, it makes comprehension harder.

100Client Management System 非原文
1You may have heard about a client management system that can collaborate clients’ data.
2If we have such a system…
3Now we have this system…
4This system can benefit …
5Once you have this system, even those people who don’t understand management can use it well.

101Validity & Reliability 非原文
1Psychologists measure results in terms of validity and reliability.
2Validity is defined as …
3For example, when a survey is asking about someone’s personality, it shouldn’t ask him chemistry questions.
4Meanwhile, a survey also values reliability.

102Hip Hop Culture 非原文
1Hip Hop culture emerged as a reaction to the gang culture and violence of the South Bronx in the 1970s, and daily experiences of poverty, racism, exclusion, crime, violence, and neglect.
2It necessarily embodies and values resilience, understanding, community and social justice.
3Without these, Hip Hop culture would never have been, and it is because these values remain at its core that Hip Hop is such a powerful agent of positive social change around the world.
4Yet, the Hip Hop project is not yet free from these difficult circumstances.

103Copernicanism 非原文
1The expending influence of Copernicanism through the seventeenth century transformed not only the natural philosophic leaning of astronomers but also the store of conceptual material accessible to writers of fiction.
2During this period of scientific revolution, a new literary genre arose, namely that of the scientific cosmic voyage.
3In do doing, they discover that these once remote world are themselves earth-like in character.
4Descriptions of these planetary bodies as terrestrial in kind demonstrate the seventeenthcentury intellectual shift from the Aristotelian to the Copernican.

104EU Fish Problems 非原文
1The European Union has two big fish problems.
2One is that, partly as a result of its failure to manage them properly, its own fisheries can no longer meet European demand.
3The other is that its governments won’t confront their fishing lobbies and decommission all the surplus boats.
4The EU has tried to solve both problems by sending its fishermen to West Africa. Since 1979 it has struck agreements with the government of Senegal, granting our fleets access to its waters.
5As a result, Senegal’s marine ecosystem has started to go the same way as ours.

105New Ventures 非原文
1New Ventures is a program that helps entrepreneurs in some of the world’s most dynamic, emerging economies– Brazil, China, Colombia, India, Indonesia and Mexico.
2We have facilitated more than $203 million in investment, and worked with 250 innovative businesses whose goods and services produce clear, measurable environmental benefits, such as clean energyefficient
3Often they also address the challenges experienced by the world’s poor.
4For example, one of the companies we work with in China, called Eco-star, refurbishes copy machines from the United States and re-sells or leases them for 20 percent less than a branded photocopier.

106Glow Worms 非原文
1The Newnes railroad was closed in 1932 after 25 years of shipping oil shale.
2The rails were pulled out of the 600-meter tunnel, which had been bored through the sandstone in the Wollemi National Park, and the tunnel was left to its own devices.
3For Newnes, that meant becoming home to thousands and thousands of glow worms.
4The glow worm is a catch-all name for the bioluminescent larvae of various species, in this case, the Arachnocampa richardsae, a type of fungus gnat.
5Found in massive numbers in caves, the fungus gnat larvae cling to the rocky walls of the abandoned tunnel and hunt with long, glowing strings of sticky mucus.

107解谜实验 非原文
1When I was a young scholar, I do a … research that change my perception…
2I invited one student from their school each time, set them comfort and then give them a puzzle.
3One is very easily, another is very hard.
4The students do the question with confuse and….
5I observe their behavior and record their strategies

108Festival in the Desert 非原文
1The “Festival in The Desert” is a celebration of the musical heritage of the Touareg, a fiercely independent nomadic people.
2It is held annually near Essakane, an oasis some 40 miles north-west of Timbuktu, the ancient city on the Niger River.
3Reaching it tests endurance, with miles of impermanent sand tracks to negotiate.
4The reward of navigating this rough terrain comes in the form of a three-day feast of music and dance.

109International Economics 非原文
1International Economics: Theory and Policy is a proven approach in which each half of the book leads with an intuitive introduction to theory and follows with self-contained chapters to cover key policy applications.
2The Eighth Edition integrates the latest research, data, and policy in hot topics such as outsourcing, economic geography, trade and environment, financial derivatives, the subprime crisis, and China’s exchange rate policies.
3New for the Eighth Edition, all end-of-chapter problems are integrated into MyEconLab, the online assessment and tutorial system that accompanies the text.
4Students get instant, targeted feedback, and instructors can encourage practice without needing to grade work by hand.

110Green Tea Health Benefits 非原文
1In May 2006, researchers at Yale University School of Medicine weighed in on the issue with a review article that looked at more than 100 studies on the health benefits of green tea.
2They pointed to what they called an “Asian paradox,” which refers to lower rates of heart disease and cancer in Asia despite high rates of cigarette smoking.
3They theorized that the 1.2 liters of green tea that is consumed by many Asians each day provides high levels of polyphenols and other antioxidants.
4These compounds may work in several ways to improve cardiovascular health.
5Specifically, green tea may prevent the oxidation of LDL cholesterol (the “bad” type), which, in turn, can reduce the buildup of plaque in arteries, the researchers wrote.

111Musical Notion 非原文
1Over the years many human endeavours have had the benefit of language.
2In particular a written language can convey a lot of information about past events, places, people and things.
3But it is difficult to describe music in words, and even more difficult to specify a tune.
4It was the development of a standard musical notation in the 11th century that allowed music to be documented in a physical form.
5Now music could be communicated efficiently, and succeeding generations would know something about the music of their ancestors.

112Taking Lecture Notes 非原文
1Your main job in taking lecture notes is to be a good listener.
2To be a good listener, you must learn to focus and concentrate on the main points of the lecture.
3Get them down, and then later reorganize them in your own words.
4Once you have done this, you have set the stage for successful reviewing and revising.

113Australia’s native plants and animals 非原文
1Australia’s native plants and animals adapted to life on an isolated continent over millions of years.
2Since European settlement they have had to compete with a range of introduced animals for habitat, food and shelter.
3Some have also had to face new predators.
4These new pressures have also caused a major impact on our country’s soil and waterways and on its native plants and animals.

114Internship 非原文
1During the school year, we had the benefit of being both unaccountable and omnipotent.
2Insulated from the consequences of such decisions, and privy to all critical information about the case, we were able to solve complex business problems with relative ease.
3We knew that once we began our internships, this would no longer be the case.
4The information would be more nebulous and the outcomes of our decisions would be unpredictable.
5So in approaching this impending summer period, what lingered in the back of our minds was a collectively felt, unspeakable thought: “Were we really up to the challenge?”

115Pidgins 非原文
1In some areas, the standard chosen may be a variety that originally had no native speakers in the country.
2For example, in Papua New Guinea, a lot of official business is conducted in Tok Pisin.
3This language is now used by over a million people, but it began many years earlier as a kind of ‘contact’ language called a pidgin.
4A pidgin is a variety of a language (e.g. English) that developed for some practical purpose, such as trading, among groups of people who had a lot of contact, but who did not know each other’s languages.

116Sea Level Rise 非原文
1Sea level rise led to 36 thousand people die every year.
2This number can be raised if sea level ceaseless goes up, scientists notified.
3According to the research, if sea level rises 50 centimeters, 86 million people will die.
4If sea level rises 1 meter, 168 million people will die all around the world.

117Fruit and Vegetable Intake 非原文
1Fruit and vegetable intake is important for the prevention of future chronic disease, so it’s important to know whether intakes of teens are approaching national objectives for fruit and vegetable consumption.
2Larson and colleagues from the University of Minnesota undertook the study to examine whether or not teens in the state were increasing their intake of fruits and vegetables.
3The study gathered information about fruit and vegetable intake among 944 boys and 1,161 girls in 1999 and again in 2004.
4Teens in middle adolescence are eating fewer fruits and vegetables than in 1999, Larson and colleagues found.
5This is giving us the message that we need new and enhanced efforts to increase fruit and vegetable intake that we haven’t been doing in the past.

118Blue Halos 非原文
1Latest research has found that several common flower species have nanoscale ridges on the surface of their petals that meddle with light when viewed from certain angles.
2These nanostructures scatter light particles in the blue to ultraviolet colour spectrum, generating a subtle effect that scientists have christened the ‘blue halo’.
3By manufacturing artificial surfaces that replicated ‘blue halos’, scientists were able to test the effect on pollinators, in this case foraging bumblebees.
4They found that bees can see the blue halo, and use it as a signal to locate flowers more efficiently.

119Japanese girl leaning English 非原文
1This paper summarizes some of the major data gathered in a longitudinal, naturalistic study of a Japanese girl learning English as a second language.
2The subject in this study is Uguisu, “nightingale” in Japanese.
3Her family came to the United States for a period of two years while her father was a visiting scholar at Harvard, and they took residence in North Cambridge, a working-class neighbourhood.
4The children in that neighbourhood were her primary source of language input.
5Uguisu also attended public kindergarten of two hours every day, and later elementary school, but with no tutoring in English syntax. Most of her neighbourhood friends were in her same class at school

010Piano 非原文
1Piano keys are generally made of spruce or basswood, for lightness.
2Spruce is normally used in high-quality pianos.
3Traditionally, the black keys were made from ebony and the white keys were covered with strips of ivory, but since ivory-yielding species are now endangered and protected by treaty, plastics are now almost exclusively used.
4Also, ivory tends to chip more easily than plastic.

067Young Employees 非原文
1Employers are often reluctant to hire young people, even though there are more than 850,000 unemployed 16-to24-year-olds and UK businesses are struggling to fill one in five vacancies because of skills
shortages.2
3But a growing number of companies are setting up schemes to recruit young workers.
4They can be surprised by the results.

074United Nations Conference 非原文
1Conferences have played a key role in guiding the work of the United Nations since its very inception.
2In fact, the world body was born when delegates from 50 nations met in San Francisco in April 1945 for the United Nations Conference on International Organization.
3The recent high-profile conferences on development issues, which have continued a series that began in the 1970s, have broken new ground in many areas: by involving Presidents, Prime Ministers and other
4These events have put long-term, difficult problems like poverty and environmental degradation at the top of the global agenda.
5These problems otherwise would not have the political urgency to grab front-page headlines and command the attention of world leaders.

076Silent Students in Tutorials 非原文
1Many students sit in a tutorial week after week without saying anything.
2Why is that?
3Maybe they do not know the purpose of a tutorial.
4They think it is like a small lecture where the tutor gives them information.
5Even if students do know what a tutorial is for, there can be other reasons why they keep quiet.

077Amino Acid 非原文
1Amino acid, which is also known as Leucine, is a fundamental element in the muscle’s formation…
2Animals’ protein has a x% of the Leucine, which is higher than those in plants’ protein.
3Plants’ protein….
4However, there are also some exceptions exist.

078Diversity 非原文
1To see whether diversity matters on the land and in the sea, …… join the forces.
2These researchers will test the full resources of …
3The data range from … and a database, to kitchen’s recorders and archaeologists.
4The results of this research will be published in science.

079The Job of a Manager 非原文
1The job of a manager in the workplace is to get things done through employees.
2In order to accomplish this, the manager should be able to motivate employees.
3That is, however, easier said than done.
4Motivation practice and theory are difficult subjects, encompassing various disciplines.

085Superpower 非原文
1The ‘superpower’ has international text, which means having control over resources … political power.
2It terms of superpower, it included …
3… ‘green superpower’, …
4In addition to green energy superpower, company should meet the above global average … emissions … and …

090伦敦不夜城 非原文
1伦敦变成不夜城
2This change 的背后……
3原因是因为街上安装了电灯
4This invention ……

091Farmlands 农耕地 非原文
1小草长出来
2然后长大了变成树苗，挡住下面的草地
3树苗又变成大树，变成森林
4如果放弃的话，就会从头再来
5Farmlands always comes from forests.

entry.title: 命令行玩转格式转换
entry.content:
多格式通用 .png .jpg .jpeg & .icon, etc,.

修改图片像素大小

convert 文件名.jpg -resize 380x278 新文件名.jpg

转为黑白图片

convert 文件名.jpg -monochrome 新文件名.jpg

转为灰色图片

convert 文件名.jpg -colorspace Gray 新文件名.jpg

转为4张不同灰色图片

convert 文件名.png -separate 新文件名.jpg

转 .pdf 为 .jpg

正常.jpg
sips -s format jpeg 文件名.pdf --out 新文件名.jpg

透明背景

sips -s format png 文件名.pdf --out 新文件名.png

Batch Processing 将所有 .png 转为: .jpg:

for i in *.png ; do convert "$i" "${i%.*}.jpg" ; done

OR

ls -1 *.png | xargs -n 1 bash -c 'convert "$0" "${0%.*}.jpg"'

OR

mogrify -format jpg *.png

OR 同时删除原文件

mogrify -format jpg *.png && rm *.png

OR 同时删除原文件

find . -iname '*.png' | while read i; do mogrify -format jpg "$i" && rm "$i"; echo "Converted $i to ${i%.*}.jpg"; done

OR 同时删除原文件

for i in *.png; do mogrify -format jpg "$i" && rm "$i"; echo "$i converted to ${i%.*}.jpg"; done

entry.title: 遗嘱咨询
entry.content:
Scenario: Mrs Lee goes to see her solicitor about making a will
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning, Mrs Lee. The interpreter is here so we can understand each other now. Could you tell me how I can help you today?25
M1我今天来是想咨询立遗嘱的事情, 我的年纪大了,觉得是时候处理这个问题了。I came here today to enquire about making a will. Considering my age, I feel it’s the right time to deal with this issue.
E2As a matter of fact, no matter how old they are, everyone should have a valid will, which makes sure that their belongings are distributed according to their wishes rather than under the rigid formula that exists for those who die intestate.42
M2说得有道理。那就请您先解答我的几个疑问吧。首先，立遗嘱应该先做什 么？遗嘱都应该包括什么呢？I agree. Could you start by answering a couple of questions for me? Firstly, what should I start with when making a will, and what should be included?
E3Firstly, you should know that a will must be accepted by a court and it must also be in writing, signed and witnessed by two witnesses.// As for the other question, all your property and possessions should be included in the will. This includes everything you own: your home, car, money, insurance policies, shares, securities, jewellery and so on.59
M3我明白了。我还有一个问题，不好意思，我对于法律方面一窍不通。是您帮 我处理和遗嘱相关的所有事宜，对吗？I see. I have another question. Sorry, I have no knowledge of law at all. So you will be the one helping me deal with all the issues related to the will, right?
E4Of course, I’m more than happy to take care of it for you. Also, you need to start considering appointing an executor to handle your affairs after you die. This can be anyone you want, including myself.37
M4我可以指定我的一个孩子做遗嘱执行人么？我有两个孩子，一儿一女。但他 们也都没有学过法律专业。执行人都有哪些职责呢？Can I appoint one of my children to be the executor? I have two children, one son and a daughter. But neither of them has studied law. What are the responsibilities of an executor?
E5The executor will be the one who has to obtain probate of the will and pay any taxes, debts or expenses before distributing the balance to the beneficiaries named in your will. So it has to be an independent party, and can’t be the beneficiaries.45
M5这样的话，那我想，我最好还是找一个专业人士吧。我可以请您做我的遗嘱 执行人吗？If that’s the case, I’d better find a professional to do this. So can I ask you to be the executor of my will?
E6Certainly! I assure you that you can trust me and my professional knowledge and experience. The first thing you need to do is to give me a list of your possessions and we can start to draft a will if you like.42
M6我在 Hurstville 有一栋房子，拥有两台车。除了银行存款以外，我还有一些股 票证券方面的投资，价值大约有 50 来万吧。其他的小额财产，我需要回家好 好整理下，再告诉您。I have a house in Hurstville and two cars. Apart from bank savings, I have investments such as shares and securities, roughly valued at $500,000. As for other small value possessions, I have to sort them out at home first before I get back to you.

entry.title: 斐济换钱
entry.content:
This is a conversation between a financial advisor and Mr Wang about exchanging money into Fijian Dollars.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1G’day Mr Wang! I’m David McDonald, financial adviser of the Commonwealth Bank of Australia. How can I help you today?20
M1你好，麦当劳先生。我今天是想来咨询一下关于我去斐济旅游的问题，我是 否需要提前在澳洲兑换一些斐济币。Good morning Mr McDonald. I’m here to inquire about my trip to Fiji. Do I need to buy some Fijian dollars here in advance?
E2Certainly you could, but as the Fijian dollar is not a common currency, we need you to make an appointment. But, let me check for you and see how much cash we have on hand. So, how much do you want?41
M2我想兑换大概价值澳元 3000 元的斐济币。大概是多少钱呢？我要去那边呆 1 个月呢，而且可能去外岛度假，所以可能会花不少钱。我怕那边太偏远，没 法用信用卡，所以我还是比较想换好现金再去。I’d like to convert about AU$3000 to Fijian dollars. How much can I get? I’ll need to stay there for about a month and I may spend some time during the holiday going to outer islands as well, so it may cost quite a lot of money. I’m worried that the place is too remote to use my credit card, so I want exchange some cash to take with me.
E3Yes, you are right. If you go to some remote islands, we would suggest that you to take enough cash to last the whole time. However, if you arrive in Fiji at Nadi International Airport, there are ATM facilities and exchange booths for traveler’s cheques and Australian dollar notes. Umm …. According to our records, we have only 1000 Fijian dollars at this branch.64
M3这不够啊！我是会在楠迪机场降落，但是我要立刻转机，可能没有时间出去 找两替店了。我知道信用卡可以用，但是我听说不论走万事达还是走维萨的 通道，都会收取高达 3%的手续费。刚才你说的旅行支票是什么？Ah that won’t be enough! It is true I will land at Nadi Airport, but I’m going to transfer straight away, so I may not have time to find an exchange booth. I know I can use my credit card, but I heard that both Visa and Master card charge a transaction fee as high as 3%. You just mentioned traveller’s cheques. What is that?
E4Yes, you are right. You will be charged at least 3% of the transaction as a processing fee. And we are not sure whether they accept American Express since we don’t have any ATMs there. As I know, ANZ has some ATMs at Nadi Airport. You can enquire with them about this. In terms of traveller’s cheques, it’s like a bank cheque, which allows you to withdraw cash overseas in a safe and reliable way.75
M4那好的，我看不必要去问 ANZ 了。我可以兑换你现有的 1000 斐济元现金 么？然后其余的 2500 澳币左右我想要旅行支票。这样我可以一降落就马上 把旅行支票兑换成当地货币。Okay. I don’t think I need to ask ANZ. Can I buy the 1000 Fijian Dollars you have now? And I’d like a travellers cheque for the remaining AU$2500 so that I can exchange it for local currency right after landing.
E5Sure, I will process this for you now. So you want all 50 dollar notes or a combination of 50 and 100 dollar notes?24
M5我想要 50 一张的纸币作为零花钱，免得到了当地老拿出大钞来，别人以为 我很有钱，抢劫我。I’d like to have 50 dollar notes as pocket money, so that I don’t have to use large notes all the time. Otherwise people may think I’m very rich and want to rob me.
E6Yes. That’s a good way to avoid any risk when you are travelling overseas. Here you are, that’s your $1000 Fijian dollar cash and your traveller’s cheque. Please keep it safe and if any is stolen or lost, please report it to us at the earliest opportunity.47
M6好的，如果真的掉了我会跟你们电话报失的。谢谢。再见。Fine. If I were to lose it I would report to you. Thank you, bye.

entry.title: 性别歧视
entry.content:
Ms. Yang is speaking to a Legal Aid officer about gender discrimination and unfair dismissal.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1你好，我想来咨询一下关于性别歧视和不公平解雇方面的问题。Hello, I am here to make an enquiry about gender discrimination and unfair dismissal.
M1Certainly. Do you feel you have been the victim of this in your work place? Would you like to tell me more about it?24
E2在被解雇前, 我在一家俱乐部做代理经理，每星期工作 20 小时。前 不久俱乐部的董事们决定将这个职位提升为全职职位。 // 可是一星 期前董事们给了我两周通告，把我辞退了， 理由是他们想找个男的 来做这份工作。我觉得这太不公平了！I was the acting manager of a club before I got dismissed. I worked there 20 hours a week. Not long ago, the club directors decided to make this role a full-time position. However, a week ago, they gave me a two- week dismissal notice, because they preferred to get a male staff member to do the job. I think it’s so unfair!
M2Based on what you’ve told me, the actions of the club directors are indeed against the law. Have you tried talking to them? If the club has a set of policies or a procedure to deal with this kind of thing, it would be advisable to try to get this problem addressed through these channels first.56
E3我已经试过和董事们沟通了，但他们不听，我也没有办法。俱乐部也没有专门应付此类事件的策略或程序 – 我是代理经理，这一点我最清楚了。I tried to talk to the directors but they wouldn’t listen to me. There’s nothing I could do about it. And the club doesn’t have any policies or procedures to deal with that kind of issue. I know that better than anyone else as a previous acting manager there.
M3What I suggest you do then is to write to the Anti-Discrimination Board, explain what happened and ask them to help you. It won’t cost you anything and you don’t need a lawyer to do it, but the complaint must be in written form, and you must sign it.49
E4可是，我的英语水平很差， 肯定看都看不懂那些表格和文件。万一 写错了什么地方，可怎么办呢？所以，您能不能替我起草一份投诉书呢？But my English is very poor. I can’t even understand those documents and forms. What if I make a mistake? Can you help me draft a complaint application?
M4You can write to the board in any language, and there are discrimination complaint forms you can obtain from their website, so you don’t have to worry about your English skills. The board staff can also help you complete the form.41
E5那太好了。那您能否现在帮我打开那个反歧视委员会的网站，然后帮我把表格打印出来呢？口译员在这里，更好可以帮我翻译。那么我投诉之后会怎样呢？That’s great. Could you now go to the website of the AntiDiscrimination Board and print out the forms for me? The interpreter here can help me translate. So what will happen after the complaint is lodged?
M5Sure. Here you are. The board will investigate your complaint, and if they find that the actions of your employer were against the law, they will try to facilitate conciliation. The settlement will be a private agreement between you and your employer, and what it entails depends on what both parties are willing to offer and accept.57
E6听起来有些复杂而且很耗时间吧？我想继续在俱乐部里做经理，您觉得这有可能吗？It sounds a bit complicated and time-consuming. I’d like to continue being a manager at the club. Do you think this would be possible?
M6It will depend on what your employers are willing to offer. They may choose to offer you an apology, financial compensation, or perhaps your old position. You will have to talk to them about it when the board organises a conciliation session.42

entry.title: 照料津贴
entry.content:
Mr Gao, a Mandarin-speaking man, has come to a local Centrelink office. He has asked to speak to a Centrelink officer about applying for carer payments.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning. Please take a seat. Before we start could I confirm your CRN (Client Reference Number) and your name please?18
M1我上个星期才拿到卡，号码是 524 094 104K.我的名字是 Qiang Gao.I only got the card last week. The number is 524 094 104K. My name is Qiang Gao.
E2Cheers, Mr. Gao. So, what can I help you with today?11
M2是关于我的孙子，他叫查斯特，今年 12 岁了。他有唐氏综合症和智障。因为 我要全职的照顾他所以我没办法工作。最糟糕的是，我的太太最近又丢了工 作。如果 Centrelink 可以给我们一些帮助我们会很感激的。It’s about my grandson, Chester. He is 12 years old. He has Down’s syndrome and an intellectual disability. Since I provide full-time care for him I am not able to work. The worst thing is, my wife has lost her job. We would be extremely grateful for any kind of assistance that Centrelink can give us.
E3Well you may be eligible for Carer Payment, and I see on your file that you and your grandson are both permanent residents. So, may I ask, when did you first arrive and settle in Australia?36
M3我们是 2005 年一月从中国移民过来的。那年七月，我们全家搬到墨尔本边上 的一个小镇上，然后就一直住到现在。We migrated here from China in January 2005. Then in July the same year, my family and I moved to a small town near Melbourne. We have been living there since then.
E4The reason I asked about when you came to Australia is because to qualify for a Carer Payment both you and the person you are looking after must be Australian residents and they must have been living in the country for at least two years. Now, I can see this would not be a problem in your case.58
M4我想这份补助真的会帮到我们很大的忙。除了查斯特的医药费以外，我们还有 汽油费，每天开两个小时的车去特殊学校。您觉得我们每个月可以拿多少钱 呢？I think this payment would help us a lot. On top of the cost of his medical expenses, there are also petrol expenses. We have to drive two hours every day to get to a special school. So how much money do you think we can get per month?
E5Well I’m afraid I won’t be able to give you an exact figure today. The Carer Payment is income and assets tested, so individual payments do vary. I would advise you to register an ‘intent to claim’ form for the Carer Payment as soon as you can.47
M5那还有别的什么补助，我有可能符合资格领取吗？请你帮我查一下好吗？Are there other kinds of payments that we may be eligible to receive? Could you find out for me?
E6Now as part of the payment you will also receive a Pensioner Concession Card and Pharmaceutical Allowance. You may also be able to get a few other payments such as Rent Assistance and Utilities Allowance. And if you are eligible you will be notified automatically. You won’t need to apply for them yourself.53
M6嗯，那个太好啦。我这就会去填表。谢谢！OK. I think I’ll go fill in the form right away. Thank you!

entry.title: 疑似失眠
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Dr. Philip, a GP, and Mrs. Li, about her sleeping problems.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good afternoon Mrs. Li. So what has brought you here today?11
M1下午好，Philip 医生。我最近感觉不太好，因为我晚上总是睡不好觉， 所以白天就无精打采的，还很易怒。Good afternoon, Doctor Philip. I’ve been feeling very well lately. I am listless and  moody during the day because I have been having sleeping problems.
E2I see. How long exactly have you been suffering from disturbed sleep? Also, could you tell me what kind of sleeping problems you have specifically?25
M2大概有三个多星期了， 就是自从我退休之后。 晚上的时候， 我觉得焦躁不安，总要在床上翻来覆去几个小时，很难入睡。It has been over three weeks probably, ever since I retired. I feel restless through the night and I often toss and turn for hours but just can’t fall asleep.
E3Sleeping problems are particularly common in people over 65. They can affect people in different ways. For example, they may have trouble falling asleep, they may wake frequently or have poor sleep quality.33
M3是呀，是呀。您说的这些症状，我都有。我该怎么办呢？医生，您说我是不是得了失眠症了呢？Yes, exactly. You see, I have all the symptoms that you just mentioned. What shoul I do? Doctor, do you think I have insomnia?
E4It depends. The need for sleep varies from person to person. It also varies depending on the level of activity. In your case, I believe the main cause is just a change of lifestyle after retirement.36
M4我的确是很难适应现在的退休生活。可是，这个问题每天都越发严重了。那您能给我开点安眠药吗？我太久都没有好好得睡上一觉了。I do have problems adapting to retirement. But this condition is getting worse day  by day. Could you prescribe some sleeping pills for me? I haven’t had a proper night’s sleep for a long time.
E5Actually, at this stage, doing more exercise and being more active during the day could be a better solution for you. Also, worrying about loss of sleep can in turn lead to having even worse sleep.36
M5好吧，既然您说不是大问题，那我就放心了。那除了您刚说的，在睡前习惯方面，我有什么需要注意的吗？Alright, I’m very much relieved since you said it’s not a big deal. Apart from what you just mentioned, is there anything else I should pay attention to in terms of pre-sleep routine?
E6Well, yes. Doing things such as spending at least half an hour winding down, reading a paper or listening to some music can be quite helpful.26
M6那好吧。我会照您的话去做的。谢谢。Alright. I will follow your advice. Thank you.

entry.title: 探亲签证
entry.content:
Mrs. Lu is being interviewed by a member of the Migration Review Tribunal (MRT) regarding a rejected visa application.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1How are you, Mrs Lu? Good to see you today. Please have a seat.14
M1謝謝，您一定得幫我。 我帮我的弟弟申请了短期停留探访签证，但 是申请遭到了拒绝。我们现在很绝望。Thank you. Please help me. I helped my brother to apply for a short stay visitor’s visa. But our application was rejected and we’re really desperate.
E2Don’t be upset. Our job in the MRT is to review unfavourable decisions made by DIAC. However, in making its decisions, it must have regard to the relevant laws, and to government policy. Does that make sense?37
M2恩，我明白。我想我来对地方了。我认为移民局的拒签決定太不合 理了。我肯定，他们错了。Yes, I understand. I think I have come to the right place. I think DIAC’s decision to reject the visa is so unfair, and I am sure they have made a mistake.
E3I can’t say anything about that until I get the details. Now, this matter relates to the refusal to grant a Short Stay visitor’s Visa. I know you wanted to sponsor your brother to come to Australia for a three-month  period. Why was that?44
M3是這樣，我母親跟我們一起住在澳洲，她一直有糖尿病，去年開始 又有了一系列的併發症，半年前還發作過一次心臟病，身體越來越 差了。她很想再見一次我的弟弟，所以我弟弟需要一个签证。Well… my mother is living with us in Australia. She has diabetes and she’s started to suffer a series of complications since last year. Her health has been getting worse since her heart attack 6 months ago. But she really wants to see my brother again, so my brother needs a visa.
E4According to the decision, they were not satisfied that your brother would leave Australia when his visa expired. The reason is firstly, that your brother has never travelled overseas before; and that he had limited incentives to return home. Why do you think the decision is incorrect?47
M4我弟弟在中國經營著一家書店， 有自己 的 房產 ， 有妻子和一個孩 子。我們已經在申請里提交了銀行證明，顯示我弟弟有超過 70 萬人 民幣的银行存款。My brother owns a bookshop and has his own property in China. He has a wife and a child. We did provide the bank statement with our application, showing that his bank savings are over 700 thousand RMB.
E5That makes sense. Looking through the file, I can see that you provided very detailed documentation with your brother’s application. And now you have provided me with a letter that states you will accept the imposition of a security bond for your brother, if necessary.45
M5是的，先生。我們是 好公民，並沒有任何不良行為。澳洲移民局爲 什麽這麼冷酷？我只是想让我的弟弟来这里探望生病的母亲而已。Yes, Sir. We are good citizens here with no record of bad behaviour. Why is DIAC so cold-hearted? We just want to bring my brother here to visit my mother as she is sick.
E6You are absolutely right. It is quite possible that the MRT will set aside the decision of DIAC.18

entry.title: INTE2047 Sample Exam
entry.content:
Section A [15分总]

Section A: Multiple-Choice Questions (total 15 marks: 15 questions, 1 mark each)

NO.问:答:
重点反选HTMLCSSJSCODE反选CODE
1Which of the following is not a markup language?PHP
2Which of the following revenue models is most appropriate for a blog that provides free reviews of products but has no shopping cart?Affiliate
3SSI stands for:Server Side Includes
4Which of the following is not one of the three basic types of cloud computing services?Web as a Service (WaaS)
5What is an advantage of using an external CSS file?All of the above
6In order from beginning to end, the major steps in the SDLC are:Systems analysis/planning; systems design; building the system; testing; and implementation.
7Which of the following statements is false?E-commerce does not directly generate revenue for the firm.
8Which of the following represents an email link in an HTML page?<a href = “mailto:xxx@yyy”>
9Which CSS statement adds a background colour to an HTML page?background-color:blue;
10Which of the following is not an example of a type of e-Commerce?P2C
11HTTP is part of which of the following TCP/IP Protocol Architecture Layers?Application
12What does the # in CSS code mean?An identifier that will be used only once.
13The key purpose for using an external CSS file is:To separate style from content on a web page.
14Which of the following is not one of the eight unique features of e-commerce technology?Offline advertising
15What is the correct way to write a multi-lined comment in JavaScript?/* */

Section B [70分总]

Fill-in-the-Blanks: (total 70 marks: 35 blanks, 2 marks each)

B.01: 30分

16.(30 pts)
(h) IP address👈🏻15个选项👇🏻
(j) Router(e) Virus
(k) DNS/ICANN(b) HTTP
(b) HTTP(c) Positioning
(o) Video(d) Mobile Device
(l) Creative(a) Niche
(m) Fieldset(g) Disintermediation
(i) Fee(f) Re-Invention
(*)答:
(a)A sub-market (or a smaller market within a bigger market) is called a Niche market.
(b)The world wide web is based upon the HTTP protocol.
(c)Another phrase for CSS layout is Positioning.
(d)A new rival to the PC for Internet access is the Mobile Device.
(e)Virus is the most common type of attack against a computer system.
(f)There are 3 evolutionary phase of e-commerce, First Wave ? Innovation, SecondWave ? Consolidation and Third Wave - Re-Invention.
(g)The elimination of intermediaries in the supply chain is called Disintermediation.
(h)Everything that connects to the Internet must have an IP address.
(i)An online travel agent is an example of a Fee based e-business model.
(j)A Router is used as junction points to direct the traffic of packets on the web.
(k)Assigns words to numeric IP addresses DNS/ICANN.
(l)Creative commons allows material from the web to be used by others free of charge.
(m)The Fieldset tag is used to group related elements in a form.
(n)The application layer of the TCP/IP layers uses the HTTP protocol.
(o)Video ads are the fastest growing form of advertising on the web.

B.02: 20分


B.03: 20分

  1. CSS code:
    • Rulesets
    • Selectors
    • Class/ID
    • Formatting text
    • Colour
    • Font
